mention.text: @cursorsupport wtf you charged me for april even though i canceled my subscription

triage.summary: User reports being charged in April despite canceling. Policy sources indicate cancellation stops future charges and takes effect at end of the current billing period; account-specific invoice/cancellation-timestamp review is still needed.

suggested_response: Public reply can cite: cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period, so a charge may still appear for the remainder of the active period. Route to hi@cursor.com for invoice review; do not request card details or sensitive billing/account info on X. Human checklist: confirm cancellation timestamp, current billing period end date, invoice/charge date, and whether the charge corresponds to an already-active period.
suggested_reply: Cancellation takes effect at the end of your current billing period, so you may still see a charge for that period even if you canceled. If you were billed after your cancellation, email hi@cursor.com and the team can review your April invoice against your cancellation timing.

sources.title: Refund policy
sources.snippet: ds. Can I cancel without a refund? Yes. You can cancel your subscription at any time to stop future charges. Cancellation takes effect at the end of your current billing period....
sources.relevanceReason: Semantic match plus terms: subscription, billing, cancel.

sources.title: Billing and payments
sources.snippet: ove a payment method after your subscription has been fully canceled and the billing period has ended....
sources.relevanceReason: Semantic match plus terms: canceled, subscription, billing.

sources.title: Cancel your subscription
sources.snippet: How do I cancel my subscription? Go to cursor.com/dashboard Click Billing and Invoices in the left sidebar Click Manage Subscription Click Cancel Subscription and confirm
sources.relevanceReason: Semantic match plus terms: subscription, billing, cancel.
